Implements the PeekLayer UI component for managing and animating text and icons in a timeline.

# Purpose
The code defines a C module for managing a `PeekLayer` in a graphical user interface, likely for a wearable device or similar platform. The `PeekLayer` is a UI component that can display text and icons, and it supports animations and transformations. The module provides functions to create, initialize, and destroy `PeekLayer` instances, as well as to set various properties such as background color, text content, and icon size. It also includes functions to manage animations, allowing the `PeekLayer` to transition between different states or appearances.

The module includes several key functions, such as [`peek_layer_create`](<#peek_layer_create>), [`peek_layer_destroy`](<#peek_layer_destroy>), and [`peek_layer_init`](<#peek_layer_init>), which handle the lifecycle of a `PeekLayer`. It also provides functions to set the text fields ([`peek_layer_set_fields`](<#peek_layer_set_fields>)), manage animations ([`peek_layer_create_play_animation`](<#peek_layer_create_play_animation>)), and configure visual properties like the background color and icon size. The code uses several external libraries and modules, as indicated by the included headers, to handle graphics operations, memory management, and event handling. The `PeekLayer` is designed to be flexible, supporting different display shapes and sizes, and it can be customized with different fonts and text alignments.
# Imports and Dependencies

- `peek_layer.h`

- `applib/ui/kino/kino_reel/scale_segmented.h`

### prv\_update\_proc<!-- {{#callable:prv_update_proc}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/timeline/peek_layer.c#L39>)

Renders a `PeekLayer` with optional background color and dot on a `Layer` using a `GContext`.
- **Inputs**:
    - `layer`: A pointer to a `Layer` object that represents the layer to update.
    - `ctx`: A pointer to a `GContext` object used for drawing operations.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Cast the `layer` to a `PeekLayer` pointer.
    - Get the bounds of the `layer` and store it in `layer_bounds`.
    - Check if the `bg_color` of `peek_layer` is not fully transparent.
    - If not transparent, set the fill color to `peek_layer->bg_color` and draw a circle or rectangle based on the display type.
    - If `peek_layer->show_dot` is true, set the fill color to black and draw a dot at the center of the layer.
- **Output**: No return value; the function updates the graphical representation of the `PeekLayer` on the given `Layer`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`GSize`](<../../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#GSize>)
    - [`grect_align`](<../../../applib/graphics/gtypes.c.md#grect_align>)
    - [`graphics_fill_oval`](<../../../applib/graphics/graphics_circle.c.md#graphics_fill_oval>)
    - [`graphics_fill_radial`](<../../../applib/graphics/graphics_circle.c.md#graphics_fill_radial>)


---
### prv\_layout\_text<!-- {{#callable:prv_layout_text}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/timeline/peek_layer.c#L70>)

Arranges and sizes text layers within a `PeekLayer` based on their content sizes and the layer's bounds.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``peek_layer``: A pointer to a `PeekLayer` structure that contains the text layers to be arranged.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Get the current graphics context using `graphics_context_get_current_context()`.
    - Set the size of the `title`, `subtitle`, and `number` text layers to match the size of the `peek_layer`'s bounds.
    - Calculate the content size of each text layer (`number`, `title`, `subtitle`) using `text_layer_get_content_size()`.
    - Initialize a `GPoint` `cursor` to position the `subtitle` text layer horizontally centered and vertically adjusted based on the maximum height of the `number` and `title` text layers.
    - Set the frame of the `subtitle` text layer using `layer_set_frame()` with adjusted position and size.
    - Adjust the `cursor` position for the `number` text layer, considering the `subtitle` height and margin, and set its frame.
    - Update the `cursor` position for the `title` text layer and set its frame with adjusted position and size.
- **Output**: No return value; modifies the frames of text layers within the `PeekLayer`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`text_layer_set_size`](<../../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_set_size>)
    - [`text_layer_get_content_size`](<../../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_get_content_size>)
    - [`layer_set_frame`](<../../../applib/ui/layer.c.md#layer_set_frame>)

### peek\_layer\_create<!-- {{#callable:peek_layer_create}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/timeline/peek_layer.c#L101>)

Allocates and initializes a `PeekLayer` structure with a specified frame.
- **Inputs**:
    - `frame`: A `GRect` structure that specifies the frame for the `PeekLayer`.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Allocate memory for a `PeekLayer` structure using `task_malloc`.
    - Check if the memory allocation was successful.
    - If successful, initialize the `PeekLayer` using [`peek_layer_init`](<../../../../../tests/stubs/stubs_peek_layer.h.md#peek_layer_init>) with the provided `frame`.
    - Return the pointer to the newly created `PeekLayer`.
- **Output**: A pointer to the newly created `PeekLayer` structure, or `NULL` if memory allocation fails.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`peek_layer_init`](<../../../../../tests/stubs/stubs_peek_layer.h.md#peek_layer_init>)

### peek\_layer\_init<!-- {{#callable:peek_layer_init}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/timeline/peek_layer.c#L118>)

Initializes a `PeekLayer` structure with specified frame and default settings, and sets up its sub-layers and properties.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``peek_layer``: A pointer to a `PeekLayer` structure to initialize.
    - ``frame``: A pointer to a [`GRect`](<../../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#GRect>) structure that defines the frame for the `PeekLayer`.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Assigns default values to `icon_offset_y`, `subtitle_margin`, and `dot_diameter` in the `PeekLayer` structure.
    - Initializes the `layer` member of `peek_layer` with the given `frame` and sets its update procedure to `prv_update_proc`.
    - Initializes the `kino_layer` with a frame adjusted by `icon_offset_y` and adds it as a child to the `peek_layer`.
    - Sets up text layers (`number`, `title`, `subtitle`) with specific fonts, colors, and alignment, and adds them as children to the `peek_layer`.
    - Calls [`peek_layer_clear_fields`](<../../../../../tests/stubs/stubs_peek_layer.h.md#peek_layer_clear_fields>) to initialize text fields with empty strings.
- **Output**: No return value; the function modifies the `peek_layer` structure in place.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`layer_set_clips`](<../../../applib/ui/layer.c.md#layer_set_clips>)
    - [`GRect`](<../../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#GRect>)
    - [`text_layer_init_with_parameters`](<../../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_init_with_parameters>)
    - [`peek_layer_clear_fields`](<../../../../../tests/stubs/stubs_peek_layer.h.md#peek_layer_clear_fields>)

### peek\_layer\_deinit<!-- {{#callable:peek_layer_deinit}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/timeline/peek_layer.c#L164>)

Deinitializes a `PeekLayer` by canceling its timer and deinitializing its components.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``peek_layer``: A pointer to a `PeekLayer` structure that needs to be deinitialized.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Cancel the `hidden_fields_timer` associated with the `peek_layer`.
    - Set the `hidden_fields_timer` to `EVENTED_TIMER_INVALID_ID`.
    - Deinitialize the `kino_layer` component of the `peek_layer`.
    - Deinitialize the `title`, `number`, and `subtitle` text layers of the `peek_layer`.
    - Deinitialize the main `layer` of the `peek_layer`.
- **Output**: No return value; the function operates directly on the `peek_layer` pointer.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`text_layer_deinit`](<../../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_deinit>)
    - [`layer_deinit`](<../../../applib/ui/layer.c.md#layer_deinit>)

### peek\_layer\_set\_frame<!-- {{#callable:peek_layer_set_frame}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/timeline/peek_layer.c#L174>)

Sets the frame for both the main layer and the kino layer of a `PeekLayer`.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``peek_layer``: A pointer to a `PeekLayer` structure whose frame will be set.
    - ``frame``: A pointer to a `GRect` structure that defines the new frame size and position.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Call [`layer_set_frame`](<../../../applib/ui/layer.c.md#layer_set_frame>) to set the frame of `peek_layer->layer` to the specified `frame`.
    - Call [`layer_set_frame`](<../../../applib/ui/layer.c.md#layer_set_frame>) to set the frame of `peek_layer->kino_layer` with a new `GRect` that has an origin at (0, `peek_layer->icon_offset_y`) and the size from `frame`.
- **Output**: No return value.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`layer_set_frame`](<../../../applib/ui/layer.c.md#layer_set_frame>)

### prv\_is\_dot\_size<!-- {{#callable:prv_is_dot_size}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/timeline/peek_layer.c#L184>)

Checks if the given `GSize` dimensions are less than or equal to a predefined dot size.
- **Inputs**:
    - `size`: A `GSize` structure containing width (`size.w`) and height (`size.h`) to check against the dot size.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Compares the width (`size.w`) of the `GSize` structure to `UNFOLD_DOT_SIZE_PX`.
    - Compares the height (`size.h`) of the `GSize` structure to `UNFOLD_DOT_SIZE_PX`.
    - Returns `true` if both width and height are less than or equal to `UNFOLD_DOT_SIZE_PX`, otherwise returns `false`.
- **Output**: Returns a boolean value: `true` if both dimensions are less than or equal to `UNFOLD_DOT_SIZE_PX`, otherwise `false`.

### peek\_layer\_set\_icon\_with\_size<!-- {{#callable:peek_layer_set_icon_with_size}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/timeline/peek_layer.c#L188>)

Sets an icon on a `PeekLayer` with a specified size and manages its animation and positioning.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``peek_layer``: A pointer to a `PeekLayer` structure where the icon will be set.
    - ``timeline_res``: A pointer to a `TimelineResourceInfo` structure that contains information about the timeline resource.
    - ``res_size``: A `TimelineResourceSize` value that specifies the size of the resource.
    - ``icon_from``: A `GRect` structure that specifies the starting position and size of the icon; if it is `GRectZero`, a default position is calculated.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls `kino_layer_set_reel` to reset the `kino_layer` of `peek_layer` with no reel and no ownership.
    - Retrieves the resource ID using `timeline_resources_get_id` and creates a `KinoReel` using `kino_reel_create_with_resource_system`.
    - Checks if `from_reel` is `NULL` and returns if it is.
    - Stores the resource information in `peek_layer->res_info`.
    - Gets the global frame of `peek_layer->kino_layer` using [`layer_get_global_frame`](<../../../applib/ui/layer.c.md#layer_get_global_frame>).
    - If `icon_from` is `GRectZero`, calculates a default centered position for `icon_from`.
    - Calculates the destination rectangle `icon_to` for the icon based on the size of `from_reel`.
    - Creates a new `KinoReel` using [`kino_reel_unfold_create`](<../../../applib/ui/kino/kino_reel/unfold.c.md#kino_reel_unfold_create>) with the calculated frames and sets its properties.
    - Sets the `kino_layer` of `peek_layer` to hidden and determines if a dot should be shown using [`prv_is_dot_size`](<#prv_is_dot_size>).
    - If a dot is to be shown, calls [`kino_reel_unfold_set_start_as_dot`](<../../../applib/ui/kino/kino_reel/unfold.c.md#kino_reel_unfold_set_start_as_dot>).
    - Sets the `kino_layer` of `peek_layer` with the new `kino_reel` and takes ownership.
- **Output**: No return value; the function modifies the `peek_layer` and its associated `kino_layer`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`layer_get_global_frame`](<../../../applib/ui/layer.c.md#layer_get_global_frame>)
    - [`grect_equal`](<../../../applib/graphics/gtypes.c.md#grect_equal>)
    - [`kino_reel_unfold_create`](<../../../applib/ui/kino/kino_reel/unfold.c.md#kino_reel_unfold_create>)
    - [`kino_reel_transform_set_from_frame`](<../../../applib/ui/kino/kino_reel/transform.c.md#kino_reel_transform_set_from_frame>)
    - [`kino_reel_transform_set_to_frame`](<../../../applib/ui/kino/kino_reel/transform.c.md#kino_reel_transform_set_to_frame>)
    - [`kino_reel_transform_set_transform_duration`](<../../../applib/ui/kino/kino_reel/transform.c.md#kino_reel_transform_set_transform_duration>)
    - [`layer_set_hidden`](<../../../applib/ui/layer.c.md#layer_set_hidden>)
    - [`prv_is_dot_size`](<#prv_is_dot_size>)
    - [`kino_reel_unfold_set_start_as_dot`](<../../../applib/ui/kino/kino_reel/unfold.c.md#kino_reel_unfold_set_start_as_dot>)

### prv\_scale\_to\_did\_stop<!-- {{#callable:prv_scale_to_did_stop}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/timeline/peek_layer.c#L243>)

Updates the `PeekLayer` to show or hide a dot based on the size of the transformed frame and resets the callbacks for the `KinoLayer`.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``kino_layer``: A pointer to the `KinoLayer` that is being transformed.
    - ``finished``: A boolean indicating whether the transformation has finished.
    - ``context``: A pointer to the `PeekLayer` context associated with the `KinoLayer`.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Retrieve the `PeekLayer` from the `context` pointer.
    - Get the destination frame of the transformation using [`kino_reel_transform_get_to_frame`](<../../../applib/ui/kino/kino_reel/transform.c.md#kino_reel_transform_get_to_frame>).
    - Check if the size of the destination frame is a dot size using [`prv_is_dot_size`](<#prv_is_dot_size>).
    - Set the `show_dot` property of `PeekLayer` based on the result of the dot size check.
    - Reset the callbacks for the `KinoLayer` to an empty `KinoLayerCallbacks` structure.
- **Output**: No return value; the function modifies the `PeekLayer` and `KinoLayer` state.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`kino_reel_transform_get_to_frame`](<../../../applib/ui/kino/kino_reel/transform.c.md#kino_reel_transform_get_to_frame>)
    - [`prv_is_dot_size`](<#prv_is_dot_size>)

Resets the `hidden_fields_timer` of a `PeekLayer` and makes its fields visible.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``data``: A pointer to a `PeekLayer` object.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Cast `data` to a `PeekLayer` pointer and assign it to `peek_layer`.
    - Set `peek_layer->hidden_fields_timer` to `EVENTED_TIMER_INVALID_ID`.
    - Call `peek_layer_set_fields_hidden(peek_layer, false)` to make the fields visible.
- **Output**: No return value.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`peek_layer_set_fields_hidden`](<#peek_layer_set_fields_hidden>)

### peek\_layer\_set\_scale\_to\_image<!-- {{#callable:peek_layer_set_scale_to_image}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/timeline/peek_layer.c#L258>)

Configures a `PeekLayer` to scale an image from one size to another with optional alignment and animation effects.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``peek_layer``: A pointer to the `PeekLayer` structure to configure.
    - ``timeline_res``: A pointer to the `TimelineResourceInfo` structure containing resource information for the target image.
    - ``res_size``: The size of the resource to use from the timeline.
    - ``icon_to``: A `GRect` structure defining the target frame for the image scaling.
    - ``align_in_frame``: A boolean indicating whether to align the target frame within the `icon_to` frame.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize `icon_from` to `GRectZero` and retrieve the previous `KinoReel` from `peek_layer`.
    - If a previous reel exists, set `icon_from` based on whether the reel has elapsed.
    - Clear the current reel from `peek_layer`.
    - Create a new `KinoReel` for the source image using `peek_layer`'s resource information.
    - If `timeline_res` is provided, create a `KinoReel` for the target image using the timeline resource information.
    - Determine the size of the image to scale based on whether `peek_layer` is showing a dot.
    - Calculate the global frame of the `kino_layer` and adjust `icon_from` if it is still `GRectZero`.
    - If `to_reel` exists and `align_in_frame` is true, align `icon_to` with the target reel's size.
    - Calculate the center points of `icon_from` and `icon_to`, and determine the target point for animation.
    - Create a segmented scale `KinoReel` with the calculated frames and set animation properties such as duration, delay, deflate, and bounce effects.
    - If `to_reel` exists, set it as the target reel for the transformation.
    - If the target size is a dot, configure the reel to end as a dot.
    - Set the new `KinoReel` to the `kino_layer` and register a callback for when the animation stops.
    - Register a timer to handle post-animation actions.
- **Output**: The function does not return a value; it modifies the `peek_layer` to animate the scaling of an image.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`kino_reel_transform_get_to_frame`](<../../../applib/ui/kino/kino_reel/transform.c.md#kino_reel_transform_get_to_frame>)
    - [`kino_reel_transform_get_from_frame`](<../../../applib/ui/kino/kino_reel/transform.c.md#kino_reel_transform_get_from_frame>)
    - [`layer_get_global_frame`](<../../../applib/ui/layer.c.md#layer_get_global_frame>)
    - [`grect_equal`](<../../../applib/graphics/gtypes.c.md#grect_equal>)
    - [`grect_align`](<../../../applib/graphics/gtypes.c.md#grect_align>)
    - [`grect_center_point`](<../../../applib/graphics/gtypes.c.md#grect_center_point>)
    - [`gpoint_add`](<../../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#gpoint_add>)
    - [`gpoint_sub`](<../../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#gpoint_sub>)
    - [`GPoint`](<../../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#GPoint>)
    - [`kino_reel_transform_set_from_frame`](<../../../applib/ui/kino/kino_reel/transform.c.md#kino_reel_transform_set_from_frame>)
    - [`kino_reel_transform_set_to_frame`](<../../../applib/ui/kino/kino_reel/transform.c.md#kino_reel_transform_set_to_frame>)
    - [`kino_reel_transform_set_transform_duration`](<../../../applib/ui/kino/kino_reel/transform.c.md#kino_reel_transform_set_transform_duration>)
    - [`kino_reel_scale_segmented_set_bounce_effect`](<../../../applib/ui/kino/kino_reel/scale_segmented.c.md#kino_reel_scale_segmented_set_bounce_effect>)
    - [`kino_reel_transform_set_to_reel`](<../../../applib/ui/kino/kino_reel/transform.c.md#kino_reel_transform_set_to_reel>)
    - [`prv_is_dot_size`](<#prv_is_dot_size>)
    - [`kino_reel_scale_segmented_set_end_as_dot`](<../../../applib/ui/kino/kino_reel/scale_segmented.c.md#kino_reel_scale_segmented_set_end_as_dot>)

    - ``icon_to``: A `GRect` structure that specifies the target rectangle to which the `PeekLayer` should scale.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Defines a constant `align_in_frame` as `true` to ensure alignment within the frame.
    - Calls the function [`peek_layer_set_scale_to_image`](<../../../../../tests/stubs/stubs_peek_layer.h.md#peek_layer_set_scale_to_image>) with `peek_layer`, `NULL` for the resource, `TimelineResourceSizeTiny` for the resource size, `icon_to` for the target rectangle, and `align_in_frame` to perform the scaling operation.
- **Output**: No return value; the function operates directly on the `peek_layer` object.

### peek\_layer\_set\_duration<!-- {{#callable:peek_layer_set_duration}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/timeline/peek_layer.c#L345>)

Sets the duration of the transformation for the `kino_layer` within a `PeekLayer`.
- **Inputs**:
    - `peek_layer`: A pointer to a `PeekLayer` structure.
    - `duration`: A 32-bit unsigned integer representing the duration of the transformation in milliseconds.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls `kino_layer_get_reel` to retrieve the `KinoReel` associated with the `kino_layer` of the `peek_layer`.
    - Passes the retrieved `KinoReel` and the `duration` to [`kino_reel_transform_set_transform_duration`](<../../../applib/ui/kino/kino_reel/transform.c.md#kino_reel_transform_set_transform_duration>) to set the transformation duration.
- **Output**: No return value.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`kino_reel_transform_set_transform_duration`](<../../../applib/ui/kino/kino_reel/transform.c.md#kino_reel_transform_set_transform_duration>)


---
### prv\_set\_visible<!-- {{#callable:prv_set_visible}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/timeline/peek_layer.c#L350>)

Sets the `kino_layer` of a `PeekLayer` to be visible and disables the `show_dot` flag.
- **Inputs**:
    - `peek_layer`: A pointer to a `PeekLayer` structure that contains the `kino_layer` to be made visible.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls [`layer_set_hidden`](<../../../applib/ui/layer.c.md#layer_set_hidden>) to set the `kino_layer` of the `peek_layer` to visible by passing `false` as the second argument.
    - Sets the `show_dot` attribute of `peek_layer` to `false`.
- **Output**: No return value.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`layer_set_hidden`](<../../../applib/ui/layer.c.md#layer_set_hidden>)


---
### peek\_layer\_create\_play\_animation<!-- {{#callable:peek_layer_create_play_animation}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/timeline/peek_layer.c#L355>)

Creates and returns an animation for a `PeekLayer` by making it visible and initiating a play animation on its `kino_layer`.
- **Inputs**:
    - `peek_layer`: A pointer to a `PeekLayer` structure that the function will operate on.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls [`prv_set_visible`](<#prv_set_visible>) to ensure the `PeekLayer` is visible.
    - Calls [`kino_layer_create_play_animation`](<../../../applib/ui/kino/kino_layer.c.md#kino_layer_create_play_animation>) with the `kino_layer` of the `PeekLayer` to create and return an animation.
- **Output**: Returns a pointer to an `ImmutableAnimation` object that represents the created play animation.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_set_visible`](<#prv_set_visible>)
    - [`kino_layer_create_play_animation`](<../../../applib/ui/kino/kino_layer.c.md#kino_layer_create_play_animation>)


---
### peek\_layer\_create\_play\_section\_animation<!-- {{#callable:peek_layer_create_play_section_animation}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/timeline/peek_layer.c#L360>)

Creates an animation for a section of a `PeekLayer` from a specified start to end time.
- **Inputs**:
    - `peek_layer`: A pointer to a `PeekLayer` structure that represents the layer to animate.
    - `from_elapsed_ms`: The starting time of the animation in milliseconds.
    - `to_elapsed_ms`: The ending time of the animation in milliseconds.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls [`prv_set_visible`](<#prv_set_visible>) to ensure the `peek_layer` is visible.
    - Calls [`kino_layer_create_play_section_animation`](<../../../applib/ui/kino/kino_layer.c.md#kino_layer_create_play_section_animation>) with the `kino_layer` of `peek_layer` and the specified time range to create the animation.
- **Output**: Returns a pointer to an `ImmutableAnimation` object representing the created animation.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_set_visible`](<#prv_set_visible>)
    - [`kino_layer_create_play_section_animation`](<../../../applib/ui/kino/kino_layer.c.md#kino_layer_create_play_section_animation>)


---
### peek\_layer\_play<!-- {{#callable:peek_layer_play}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/timeline/peek_layer.c#L368>)

Makes the `PeekLayer` visible and starts playing its associated `KinoLayer` animation.
- **Inputs**:
    - `peek_layer`: A pointer to a `PeekLayer` structure that contains the layer to be made visible and animated.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls [`prv_set_visible`](<#prv_set_visible>) to make the `peek_layer` visible.
    - Invokes `kino_layer_play` to start the animation of the `kino_layer` within the `peek_layer`.
- **Output**: No return value.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_set_visible`](<#prv_set_visible>)


---
### peek\_layer\_get\_size<!-- {{#callable:peek_layer_get_size}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/timeline/peek_layer.c#L373>)

Retrieves the size of the `kino_reel` associated with a `PeekLayer`.
- **Inputs**:
    - `peek_layer`: A pointer to a `PeekLayer` structure from which to retrieve the size.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls `kino_layer_get_reel` to get the `kino_reel` from the `kino_layer` within the `peek_layer`.
    - Calls `kino_reel_get_size` with the retrieved `kino_reel` to get its size.
    - Returns the size obtained from `kino_reel_get_size`.
- **Output**: Returns a `GSize` structure representing the size of the `kino_reel`.


---
### prv\_set\_text<!-- {{#callable:prv_set_text}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/timeline/peek_layer.c#L377>)

Sets the text of a `PeekTextLayer` by copying the input string into its text buffer and updating the associated text layer.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``peek_text_layer``: A pointer to a `PeekTextLayer` structure where the text will be set.
    - ``text``: A constant character pointer to the text string to be copied into the `PeekTextLayer`.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Clear the `text_buffer` of `peek_text_layer` by setting all its bytes to zero using `memset`.
    - Copy the input `text` into `peek_text_layer->text_buffer` using `strncpy`, ensuring not to exceed `MAX_PEEK_LAYER_TEXT_LEN - 1` characters.
    - Update the text of `peek_text_layer->text_layer` with the contents of `peek_text_layer->text_buffer` using [`text_layer_set_text`](<../../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_set_text>).
- **Output**: No return value; the function modifies the `PeekTextLayer` in place.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`text_layer_set_text`](<../../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_set_text>)


---
### peek\_layer\_set\_fields<!-- {{#callable:peek_layer_set_fields}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/timeline/peek_layer.c#L383>)

Sets the text fields of a `PeekLayer` and updates their layout.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``peek_layer``: A pointer to a `PeekLayer` structure that contains the text fields to update.
    - ``number``: A string representing the number text to set; can be `NULL` to leave unchanged.
    - ``title``: A string representing the title text to set; can be `NULL` to leave unchanged.
    - ``subtitle``: A string representing the subtitle text to set; can be `NULL` to leave unchanged.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Check if `number` is not `NULL`, then call [`prv_set_text`](<#prv_set_text>) to set the `number` field of `peek_layer`.
    - Check if `title` is not `NULL`, then call [`prv_set_text`](<#prv_set_text>) to set the `title` field of `peek_layer`.
    - Check if `subtitle` is not `NULL`, then call [`prv_set_text`](<#prv_set_text>) to set the `subtitle` field of `peek_layer`.
    - Call [`prv_layout_text`](<#prv_layout_text>) to update the layout of the text fields in `peek_layer`.
- **Output**: No return value; the function modifies the `peek_layer` structure in place.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_set_text`](<#prv_set_text>)
    - [`prv_layout_text`](<#prv_layout_text>)


---
### peek\_layer\_clear\_fields<!-- {{#callable:peek_layer_clear_fields}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/timeline/peek_layer.c#L397>)

Clears the text fields of a `PeekLayer` by setting them to empty strings.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``peek_layer``: A pointer to a `PeekLayer` structure whose fields will be cleared.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls the [`peek_layer_set_fields`](<../../../../../tests/stubs/stubs_peek_layer.h.md#peek_layer_set_fields>) function with the `peek_layer` and three empty strings as arguments.
- **Output**: No output is returned.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`peek_layer_set_fields`](<../../../../../tests/stubs/stubs_peek_layer.h.md#peek_layer_set_fields>)


---
### peek\_layer\_set\_fields\_hidden<!-- {{#callable:peek_layer_set_fields_hidden}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/timeline/peek_layer.c#L401>)

Sets the visibility of the text layers in a `PeekLayer`.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``peek_layer``: A pointer to a `PeekLayer` structure whose text layers' visibility will be set.
    - ``hidden``: A boolean value indicating whether to hide (`true`) or show (`false`) the text layers.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls [`layer_set_hidden`](<../../../applib/ui/layer.c.md#layer_set_hidden>) on the `number.text_layer.layer` of `peek_layer` with the `hidden` parameter.
    - Calls [`layer_set_hidden`](<../../../applib/ui/layer.c.md#layer_set_hidden>) on the `title.text_layer.layer` of `peek_layer` with the `hidden` parameter.
    - Calls [`layer_set_hidden`](<../../../applib/ui/layer.c.md#layer_set_hidden>) on the `subtitle.text_layer.layer` of `peek_layer` with the `hidden` parameter.
- **Output**: No return value.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`layer_set_hidden`](<../../../applib/ui/layer.c.md#layer_set_hidden>)

- **Inputs**:
    - ``peek_layer``: A pointer to a `PeekLayer` object whose number field is to be set.
    - ``number``: A constant character pointer representing the number to set in the `PeekLayer`.

    - Calls [`peek_layer_set_fields`](<../../../../../tests/stubs/stubs_peek_layer.h.md#peek_layer_set_fields>) with `peek_layer`, `number`, and null values for title and subtitle.
- **Output**: No return value.

[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/timeline/peek_layer.c#L411>)

Sets the title of a `PeekLayer` by updating its fields with the given title.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``peek_layer``: A pointer to a `PeekLayer` structure that will have its title set.
    - ``title``: A constant character pointer to the title string to set for the `PeekLayer`.

    - Calls the [`peek_layer_set_fields`](<../../../../../tests/stubs/stubs_peek_layer.h.md#peek_layer_set_fields>) function with `peek_layer`, `NULL` for the number, the provided `title`, and `NULL` for the subtitle.
- **Output**: No return value; the function modifies the `PeekLayer` structure in place.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`peek_layer_set_fields`](<../../../../../tests/stubs/stubs_peek_layer.h.md#peek_layer_set_fields>)

    - ``peek_layer``: A pointer to a `PeekLayer` structure where the subtitle will be set.

### peek\_layer\_set\_title\_font<!-- {{#callable:peek_layer_set_title_font}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/timeline/peek_layer.c#L419>)

Sets the font for the title text layer of a `PeekLayer` and updates its layout.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``peek_layer``: A pointer to a `PeekLayer` structure whose title font is to be set.
    - ``font``: A `GFont` object representing the new font to be applied to the title text layer.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls [`text_layer_set_font`](<../../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_set_font>) to set the font of the `title.text_layer` within the `peek_layer` to the specified `font`.
    - Invokes [`prv_layout_text`](<#prv_layout_text>) to adjust the layout of the text layers within the `peek_layer` after the font change.
- **Output**: No return value; the function modifies the `peek_layer` in place.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`text_layer_set_font`](<../../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_set_font>)
    - [`prv_layout_text`](<#prv_layout_text>)

### peek\_layer\_set\_subtitle\_font<!-- {{#callable:peek_layer_set_subtitle_font}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/timeline/peek_layer.c#L424>)

Sets the font and margin for the subtitle text layer in a `PeekLayer` and updates its layout.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``peek_layer``: A pointer to a `PeekLayer` structure that contains the subtitle text layer to modify.
    - ``font``: A `GFont` object that specifies the new font to use for the subtitle text layer.
    - ``margin``: An `int16_t` value that specifies the margin to apply to the subtitle text layer.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls [`text_layer_set_font`](<../../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_set_font>) to set the font of the `subtitle.text_layer` in the `peek_layer` to the specified `font`.
    - Sets the `subtitle_margin` of the `peek_layer` to the specified `margin`.
    - Calls [`prv_layout_text`](<#prv_layout_text>) to update the layout of the text layers in the `peek_layer`.
- **Output**: No return value; the function modifies the `peek_layer` in place.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`text_layer_set_font`](<../../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_set_font>)
    - [`prv_layout_text`](<#prv_layout_text>)

[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/timeline/peek_layer.c#L430>)

Sets the `dot_diameter` property of a `PeekLayer` object.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``peek_layer``: A pointer to a `PeekLayer` object whose `dot_diameter` property will be set.
    - ``dot_diameter``: An unsigned 8-bit integer representing the new diameter of the dot.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Assigns the value of `dot_diameter` to the `dot_diameter` field of the `peek_layer` object.
- **Output**: No return value.

    - ``icon_offset_y``: An `int16_t` value representing the new vertical offset for the icon.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Assigns the value of `icon_offset_y` to the `icon_offset_y` field of the `peek_layer` structure.
- **Output**: No return value.
